prompt: The Anthropic token-count cache in @prune/tokenizer returns WRONG counts: two clearly different prompts can come back with the identical cached count of an unrelated earlier call. Observed behavior: change the content of a nested message field (e.g. the text inside messages[].content) and the reported token count does not change — the second call is served from the cache entry of the first. Counts must be cached per distinct payload: any change to nested message content must yield a different cache entry, while a byte-identical repeat payload still hits the cache.
notes: Reference: four-eyes review fix 2026-05-30, restricted to the tokenizer package (canonicalize() passed a keys array as JSON.stringify's property filter, stripping nested keys). Replaces the dropped ob-waterbed-usd-rounding task, whose reference regression test passed at base (verification caught it).
